fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: New Jersey Pharmacist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a pharmacist? Here's whether your license transfers to New Jersey - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: pharmacist license reciprocity new jersey, how to transfer pharmacist license to new jersey, pharmacist compact new jersey, new jersey pharmacist endorsement application

board.name: New Jersey Board of Pharmacy
board.parent_dept: New Jersey Division of Consumer Affairs
board.mailing_address:
New Jersey Board of Pharmacy
P.O. Box 45013
Newark, New Jersey 07101

license_types.title: RPh, PharmD
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a pharmacist in New Jersey.

steps.title: Create an Online Account
steps.description: Register and create an account on the New Jersey Professional/Occupational Licensing System (MyLicense) portal to begin your application.

steps.title: Complete and Submit Online Application
steps.description: Log in to MyLicense and complete the pharmacist licensure application form, paying the associated application fee as instructed within the system.

steps.title: Register for and Pass Required Examinations
steps.description: Register with the National Association of Boards of Pharmacy (NABP) to take and successfully pass both the North American Pharmacist Licensure Examination (NAPLEX) and the Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE).

steps.title: Submit Official Transcripts
steps.description: Arrange for your official pharmacy school transcripts to be sent directly from your educational institution to the New Jersey Board of Pharmacy.

steps.title: Complete Criminal History Background Check
steps.description: Follow the instructions provided by the Board to complete a fingerprint-based criminal history background check through IdentoGO.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NAPLEX and MPJE.

provisional_text: Some states offer an intern or provisional license for graduates actively accumulating required hours or waiting to take the NAPLEX.
reciprocity_text: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

faqs.question: How much does a New Jersey pharmacist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies by state. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is the MPJE required in all states?
faqs.answer: Almost all states require passing their state-specific Multistate Pharmacy Jurisprudence Examination (MPJE) to verify knowledge of local pharmacy laws.

faqs.question: Does New Jersey have reciprocity for pharmacists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacist practice in New Jersey. To work in New Jersey, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacist license to New Jersey?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in New Jersey while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Pharmacists licensed in another state can often apply via license transfer (reciprocity) through the NABP, though most states still require passing their specific MPJE.

hero.title: Become a Pharmacist in New Jersey
hero.dek: To apply for an initial pharmacist license in New Jersey, create an account and submit your application through the New Jersey Professional/Occupational Licensing System (MyLicense) portal.

variants.label: New Applicant
variants.description: How to become licensed/certified for the first time

variants.label: Reciprocity
variants.description: How to transfer your existing credential from another state

variants.label: Renewal
variants.description: How to renew your existing credential

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the MyLicense portal, click 'Register here' or 'Don't have an account? Register Now', and follow the prompts to create a user ID and password to set up your account.
